text:
Databases are essential for storing and managing information. The two main types are SQL and NoSQL databases. SQL databases, like MySQL, use tables with rows and columns to organize data. They require a fixed data model where you define the structure beforehand. NoSQL databases, such as MongoDB, are more flexible, storing data in documents or key-value pairs. 

To retrieve information, you write queries. In SQL, queries use commands like SELECT and WHERE. Indexes are special lookup tables that speed up these queries by helping the database find data faster. Data modeling is the process of planning how your data will be structured, whether using tables or flexible documents, to ensure efficiency and accuracy.
